summary: 7W / 6L (53.8%) on the 13 decided matchups; PIT@NYY and BAL@BOS were postponed. Of the four real-stake GOLD CONFIRMs, results split 2-2: SD@ATL and MIA@HOU won, SF@KC and DET@CHC lost, with the two losers justified by the same 'four-dimension CAP10 rescue' logic that also produced the day's cleanest win (SD@ATL). Two DOWNGRADEs (TB@TOR on flat-edge reasoning, CHW@TEX on a single-prior-day overfit) each missed 10+ run blowout wins, and STL@LAA's code-level SKIP missed a real win for the second straight day. Directionally the model out-called Claude's suppressions more than it lost from them.

MIN @ CLE.headline: CLE beat MIN 5-2; CONFIRM SKIP correctly withheld any stake on a bottom-bucket marginal favorite that lost.
MIN @ CLE.hypothesis: fair_prob=0.409 sat below the 0.42 viability floor independent of other signals, and CAP 10 zeroed the bottom-bucket pick_prob=0.538 favorite; PQI (Δ=+17.6) also opposed MIN. MIN's loss validates the SKIP cleanly. No process failure — the fair-prob floor and CAP 10 both worked as designed.
MIN @ CLE.signals_to_recheck: fair_prob<0.42 viability floor and [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket marginal-favorite — both correctly suppressed a loser; no revision needed.

LAD @ PHI.headline: LAD edged PHI 2-1; CONFIRM SKIP was zero-staked correctly even though the direction happened to be right.
LAD @ PHI.hypothesis: SP xERA (Wheeler), PQI (Δ=+15.7), and f5_full_delta=0.5611 (the slate's largest Stage 1/2 split) all opposed LAD, yet LAD won by 1 run inside the calibration-caution edge band (23.27pp). SKIP sizing was correct regardless of direction — a 1-run win against triple-opposing signals is variance, not evidence the dual-opposing rule should become a fade.
LAD @ PHI.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta (0.5611) and calibration_caution_18_25pp — both correctly zeroed a stake on a game that easily could have gone the other way by more; consistency with the suppression-only rule, not a signal to promote to directional fade.

TB @ TOR.headline: TB blew out TOR 12-2 — the flat-edge DOWNGRADE to SKIP missed a real GOLD win by a 10-run margin.
TB @ TOR.hypothesis: Process failure. Claude downgraded on the reasoning that a 0.70pp edge means 'the market has fully priced' Gausman's advantage — an unvalidated heuristic with no prior track record, applied in direct tension with the 7/20 postgame's explicit mandate to lean CONFIRM on GOLD absent a suppression rule proven ≥70% on that exact shape. The underlying pre-cap stack was clean (SP xERA agrees, PQI confirms, F-signal fires); TB's 10-run blowout shows the flat-edge read was simply wrong, not just unlucky.
TB @ TOR.signals_to_recheck: 'flat-edge market-priced' DOWNGRADE reasoning — a novel, untracked suppression heuristic that just missed a 10-run win; treat edge_pp near the CAP boundaries as informational only until it accumulates its own hit-rate data, and do not let it override the 7/20 lean-CONFIRM mandate.

SD @ ATL.headline: SD routed ATL 8-3 — the CAP10 CONFIRM-rescue on a PLATINUM-demoted favorite paid off with a real stake.
SD @ ATL.hypothesis: Process success. Pre-cap B+ logic (F3=2777.6, well above the 1000 anchor; PQI confirms; Stage agrees) cleared the bar Claude set for rescuing a bottom-bucket favorite (pick_prob=0.538) out of CAP 10's default zero. SD's 5-run win validates both the PLATINUM-demotion scrutiny and the F3-above-1000 anchor requirement.
SD @ ATL.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 10] CONFIRM-rescue with F3_swing_take_gap>1000 as qualifier — worked here; the three real-stake CAP10-rescue CONFIRMs today (SD@ATL, SF@KC, DET@CHC) went 1-2 collectively, so keep tracking cumulative rescue hit rate before scaling further.

SF @ KC.headline: KC edged SF 3-2 — a real-stake CAP10-rescue CONFIRM lost by 1 run.
SF @ KC.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate, though the margin was thin. The 'four-dimension rescue' (SP xERA agrees, PQI confirms Δ=-14.5, Stage agrees, independent of starred F1_xera_gap=1.01*) cleared Claude's bar citing the CLE@MIA precedent, but F3=822.2 sat below the 1000-anchor threshold used to justify SD@ATL's rescue the same day — a weaker corroboration profile that still got staked. Combined with DET@CHC's larger loss on identical justification, the rescue rule went 1-for-3 on today's real stakes.
SF @ KC.signals_to_recheck: four-dimension CAP-10 rescue (CLE@MIA precedent) — 1-for-3 on today's real-stake applications; F3<1000 corroboration (822.2 here) may be a weaker rescue qualifier than F3>1000 (2777.6 at the day's only win, SD@ATL) — consider requiring F3>1000 specifically before staking a CAP10 rescue rather than accepting any independent SP+PQI+Stage combination.

NYM @ MIL.headline: NYM shut out MIL 4-0 — the sub-1000-F3 DOWNGRADE correctly protected against a real loss.
NYM @ MIL.hypothesis: Process success. F3=243.9 was well below the 1000 meaningful-anchor threshold, PQI opposed MIL (Δ=-9.0), and acute_roster plus confidence_downgrade both fired. MIL being shut out validates the DOWNGRADE cleanly — a genuinely thin B- stack, not a caps-suppressed strong pick, exactly as the reasoning distinguished.
NYM @ MIL.signals_to_recheck: sub-1000 F3_swing_take_gap as a non-anchor — correctly flagged a losing GOLD pick today; archive continues to support F3<1000 alone as insufficient for a GOLD-tier stake.

DET @ CHC.headline: CHC blew out DET 11-2 — a real-stake, four-dimension CAP10-rescue CONFIRM lost badly.
DET @ CHC.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate. The reasoning cited a genuine four-dimension stack (F1=1.82 not starred, F3=1761.1 above 1000, SP edge 1.13 agrees, PQI confirms Δ=-13.2, Stage agrees) with no Statcast redundancy concern — on paper the strongest of today's three CAP10-rescue CONFIRMs. A 9-run loss against that profile points more toward an unforeseeable in-game information gap (CHC's offense/Valdez outperforming projection) than a flawed rescue rule, but the magnitude is large enough to log alongside SF@KC as a second same-day loss on the identical justification.
DET @ CHC.signals_to_recheck: four-dimension CAP-10 rescue — second same-day real-stake loss (after SF@KC) despite F3>1000 and all four dimensions agreeing; check hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap and pen_strain_pick_side retroactively for whether CHC's offensive outperformance was foreseeable pre-game.

CHW @ TEX.headline: TEX shut out CWS 10-0 in the identical Rocker-vs-Schultz matchup — yesterday's OVERRIDE-loss lesson, applied as a blanket DOWNGRADE, missed a 10-run win.
CHW @ TEX.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate, and a cautionary tale about over-generalizing from n=1. Yesterday's real-money OVERRIDE of CAP 7 in this exact matchup lost 10-3, and avoiding a repeat override was reasonable — but a single-game loss got converted into a blanket 'archive rule' that DOWNGRADEd today's otherwise-strong GOLD pick (F1*=2.04, SP edge 1.31 agrees, PQI confirms +14.9) to SKIP. TEX won by 10 today. The underlying CAP7 sub-0.02-band pattern remains net-positive across the broader archive; one bad override should not flip the default posture on the very next occurrence without more data.
CHW @ TEX.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 7] pick-side bullpen disadvantage sub-0.02 band — now 7-1 in the broader archive (adds today's suppressed win) but 0-1 on real-money conversion (7/20 OVERRIDE loss); swinging from 'override on starter reputation' to 'blanket DOWNGRADE' cost a 10-run win the very next day. A middle path (half-Kelly sizing) may serve better than alternating extremes game to game.

MIA @ HOU.headline: HOU beat MIA 5-3 — a clean real-stake CONFIRM on an already-penalized B-/score=1 stack came through.
MIA @ HOU.hypothesis: Process success. F3=1270 and F5=+1.14 provided multi-signal support with pick_prob=0.5991 outside the bottom bucket, and the model had already sized this down from PLATINUM to reflect PQI's mild opposition (Δ=-8.3). HOU's win validates both the underlying signals and the judgment that BAL-series caution from 7/17-7/19 didn't transfer to a structurally different MIA opponent.
MIA @ HOU.signals_to_recheck: None — F3/F5 multi-signal GOLD with pick_prob outside the bottom bucket converted cleanly; no revision needed.

WSH @ COL.headline: COL walked off WSH 8-7 — the edge-floor-enforced SKIP (directional CONFIRM, tier zeroed) avoided a real loss.
WSH @ COL.hypothesis: Process success, and a useful contrast with 7/20's identical WSH@COL matchup (WSH won 7-3 that day after a DOWNGRADE that missed the win). Today the binding constraint was the same sub-4pp edge floor (2.53pp), but this time WSH lost — the code-level edge floor has now been wrong once (7/20) and right once (7/21) on the exact same matchup shape, arguing for treating it as two-way noise at this edge magnitude rather than a directional tell either way.
WSH @ COL.signals_to_recheck: edge-floor-4pp binding — now 1-1 across 7/20 (missed a WSH win) and 7/21 (avoided a WSH loss) on the identical WSH@COL matchup; continue treating sub-4pp edge as a pure sizing/variance zone, not a quality signal about the pick's direction.

STL @ LAA.headline: LAA beat STL 5-1 — CAP7 and the 15pp edge ceiling suppressed a real stake on a directionally-perfect pick for the second straight day.
STL @ LAA.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate on sizing, not direction. F1=2.49 not starred, SP edge 1.97, and PQI +34.2 (the slate's strongest confirmation) were exactly right — LAA won by 4. The tier was zeroed purely by CAP 7 (gap=0.0080, sub-0.02 band) and the edge ceiling (17.77pp>15pp), both code-level gates rather than signal doubt. This is the second consecutive day (7/20 LAA won 3-2 suppressed; 7/21 LAA won 5-1 suppressed) these exact gates zeroed a real stake on a game the qualitative signals called correctly.
STL @ LAA.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 7] sub-0.02 band and the 15pp edge ceiling — now suppressed two consecutive STL@LAA wins (7/20 3-2, 7/21 5-1) despite the slate's strongest PQI confirmation both days; worth testing whether an exceptionally strong PQI/SP-edge combination (PQI>+30, SP edge>1.5) should earn a partial-Kelly carve-out from the edge ceiling.

OAK @ ARI.headline: ARI beat OAK 6-5 — the sp_savant_gate HARD_VETO SKIP was directionally right, though the veto is a data-availability gate, not a prediction.
OAK @ ARI.hypothesis: Kohl Drake's 0-Statcast-pitch current-season sample triggered a non-negotiable HARD_VETO independent of direction; ARI winning is a favorable but incidental outcome relative to the veto's purpose. No process failure or success to score beyond confirming the veto continues functioning correctly on a genuine data void.
OAK @ ARI.signals_to_recheck: sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO on thin_sp_data — continues to function correctly as a non-directional data-availability gate; no revision needed.

CIN @ SEA.headline: CIN beat SEA 4-2 — the dual-opposing D-grade SKIP correctly avoided a real loss.
CIN @ SEA.hypothesis: Process success. SP xERA (Castillo, 1.39 for CIN) and PQI (Δ=-19.6) both opposed SEA, and CIN's win validates the read. Per the settled archive rule this remains suppression-only (zero stake), not grounds for a directional CIN fade, but the opposing direction happened to be right again.
CIN @ SEA.signals_to_recheck: SP_xERA/PQI dual-opposing at grade D — correctly predicted the loser again; consistent with treating this as suppression-only, not a fade trigger.

PIT @ NYY.headline: Game postponed; no result to grade.
PIT @ NYY.hypothesis: PIT @ NYY did not complete on 2026-07-21 — no outcome to analyze.

BAL @ BOS.headline: Game postponed; no result to grade.
BAL @ BOS.hypothesis: BAL @ BOS did not complete on 2026-07-21 — no outcome to analyze.

patterns_observed: The 'four-dimension CAP10-rescue' CONFIRM logic (CLE@MIA precedent) went 1-for-3 on today's real stakes: SD@ATL (F3=2777.6, WIN) vs SF@KC (F3=822.2, LOSS) and DET@CHC (F3=1761.1, LOSS). F3>1000 alone doesn't fully separate winners from losers here (DET@CHC had it and still lost), so track cumulative rescue hit-rate closely before scaling stake size further on this exact justification.

Two DOWNGRADEs today (TB@TOR flat-edge, CHW@TEX single-prior-day overfit) missed blowout wins of 10+ runs by inventing or over-applying unvalidated suppression logic — directly in tension with the 7/20 mandate to lean CONFIRM on GOLD absent a ≥70%-validated rule. Treat 'market has priced it in' (flat edge) as informational only, and require multiple same-shape instances before generalizing a single prior-day loss into a blanket no-override rule.

CAP 7's sub-0.02 bullpen-gap band plus the 15pp edge ceiling have now suppressed a real STL@LAA stake for two consecutive days (7/20 LAA won 3-2, 7/21 LAA won 5-1) on the slate's strongest PQI confirmation both times. Consider a carve-out allowing partial-Kelly sizing past the edge ceiling when PQI confirms at >+30 alongside SP-edge>1.5, rather than zeroing to SKIP outright.

The WSH@COL sub-4pp edge floor is now split 1-1 across two consecutive days on the identical matchup (missed a win 7/20, avoided a loss 7/21) — treat sub-4pp edge as a pure stake-sizing/variance zone with no directional information, and stop citing this exact matchup's prior-day result as evidence for either a CONFIRM or a fade.
